zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Burgh於1340年出生在英國斯塔福爾格諾薩爾時，她的父親Adam de Burgh20歲，母親Alilitha de Harcourt4歲。她和Sir John Knightley至少有5個兒子。她去世於1416年在她的家鄉享年76歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Burgh于1340年出生在英国斯塔福爾格諾薩爾时，她的父亲Adam de Burgh20岁，母亲Alilitha de Harcourt4岁。她和Sir John Knightley至少有5个儿子。她去世于1416年在她的家乡享年76岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Burgh was born in 1340, in Gnosall, Staffordshire, England, her father, Adam de Burgh, was 20 and her mother, Alilitha de Harcourt, was 4. She had at least 5 sons with Sir John Knightley. She died in 1416, in her hometown, at the age of 76, and was buried in Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Burgh est née en 1340, à Gnosall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam de Burgh, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alilitha de Harcourt, avait 4 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils avec Sir John Knightley. Elle est décédée en 1416, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth de Burgh 1340 in Gnosall, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adam de Burgh 20 und ihre Mutter Alilitha de Harcourt 4. Sie hatte mit Sir John Knightley mindestens 5 Söhne. Sie starb 1416 im Alter von 76 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Burgh è nata il nel 1340 nel Gnosall, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Adam de Burgh, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Alilitha de Harcourt, aveva 4 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli con Sir John Knightley. Morì nel 1416, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burghが1340年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアグノサルで生まれた時，父Adam de Burghは20歳，母Alilitha de Harcourtは4歳であった。彼女はSir John Knightleyとの間に少なくとも5人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1416年に故郷で76歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Burgh(이)가 1340년 영국 스태퍼드실르 뇰사에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam de Burgh(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alilitha de Harcourt(은)는 4세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Knightley(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1416년 그녀의 고향에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

mn.Gnosall, Staffordshire, England, United: Gnosall, Stæffordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth de Burgh nasceu em 1340, em Gnosall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam de Burgh, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Alilitha de Harcourt, tinha 4 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos com Sir John Knightley. Ela faleceu em 1416, em sua cidade natal, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Burgh родилась в 1340 г. в местности гносалл, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Adam de Burgh было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Alilitha de Harcourt, 4. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей от Sir John Knightley. Она умерла в 1416 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности стаффордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth de Burgh nació en 1340, en Gnosall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam de Burgh, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Alilitha de Harcourt, tenía 4 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos con Sir John Knightley. Murió en 1416, en su pueblo, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
